notes: Untiered Level 2 linear qualification designed for high-achieving students expected to achieve grades 7-9 in GCSE Mathematics. Any content from any part of the specification may be assessed on either paper. Assumes prior knowledge of KS4 Programme of Study.

papers.name: Paper 1
papers.description: Non-calculator paper. Content from any part of the specification may be assessed. Mix of question styles from short single-mark to multi-step problems.

papers.name: Paper 2
papers.description: Calculator paper. Content from any part of the specification may be assessed. Mix of question styles from short single-mark to multi-step problems.

subtopics.name: Number - fractions, decimals, percentages, standard form, surds, indices
Product rule for counting
Surds - simplifying, expanding brackets with surds, rationalising the denominator
Basic processes of algebra
Definition of a function - function notation, domain and range
Expanding brackets and collecting like terms
Factorising - common factors, difference of two squares, quadratics
Manipulation of rational expressions - algebraic fractions
Use and manipulation of formulae and expressions
Use of the factor theorem for integer values including cubics
Completing the square
Sketching functions - linear and quadratic functions
Solution of linear and quadratic equations
Algebraic and graphical solution of simultaneous equations (linear/linear or linear/quadratic)
Solution of linear and quadratic inequalities
Index laws including fractional and negative indices
Algebraic proof
nth terms of linear and quadratic sequences
Limiting values of a sequence as n→∞
Binomial expansion
Polynomial algebra including factor theorem
Gradient of a straight line joining two points
Equation of a straight line in forms y = mx + c, y - y₁ = m(x - x₁), ax + by + c = 0
Equation of a line through two points or with given gradient through a point
Conditions for parallel and perpendicular lines
Equation of a circle centre origin: x² + y² = r²
Equation of a circle centre (a,b): (x-a)² + (y-b)² = r²
Equation of tangent to a circle at a given point
Perpendicular distance from a point to a line
Differentiation of polynomials
Finding gradients of tangents and normals
Finding higher derivatives
Finding minima and maxima (stationary points)
Increasing and decreasing functions
Integration of polynomials
Finding areas under curves using integration
Applications of differentiation and integration
Matrix operations - addition, subtraction, multiplication of 2×2 matrices
Multiplication of a matrix by a scalar
Identity matrix and zero matrix
Transformations of the plane using matrices - reflections, rotations, enlargements
Successive transformations
Finding the matrix representing a given transformation
Geometric proof - formal geometric arguments
Trigonometry in triangles including sine and cosine rules
Pythagoras theorem
Trigonometric ratios of angles and their graphs - sin, cos, tan
Trigonometric identities including sin²θ + cos²θ = 1
Solving trigonometric equations
Circle theorems
Area of triangle using ½ab sin C
